question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request deduplication in MempoolTransaction() to send the same transaction identifier in thousands of concurrent requests, and if any upstream load balancer or proxy caches the response, cause all future lookups for that transaction ID to return ErrNotImplemented even after the endpoint is implemented, permanently hiding that transaction from mempool visibility?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ErrNotImplemented response from Mempool() to cause a Rosetta block-production monitor to disable its mempool-based double-spend detection, allowing the attacker to submit two conflicting transactions to different Hashgraph nodes simultaneously, with one being confirmed and the other creating an inconsistency in the distributed history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ErrNotImplemented response from MempoolTransaction() to disable a Rosetta client's transaction-status polling, causing the client to lose track of a submitted transaction's lifecycle and re-submit it after the original is confirmed, introducing a duplicate transaction into Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any TLS client certificate validation on the Mempool() endpoint (no auth fields on mempoolAPIService) to impersonate a legitimate Rosetta client and use the ErrNotImplemented response to map the mirror node's API surface, identifying unprotected history-manipulation endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any request signing or HMAC validation on MempoolTransaction() to send forged requests that appear to originate from a trusted Rosetta client, using the ErrNotImplemented response to confirm the endpoint's behavior and then pivot to forging requests on history-sensitive endpoints?
